${player} now places up to ${cube_count} cubes and scores.
${player} wins ${company_name} ${share_type_string} and pays $${PROPERTY current_bid OF current_stock_holder} [ ${previous_money} -> ${new_total} ]
The current bid of ${bid_value} is not greater than the winning bid of ${PROPERTY current_bid OF current_stock_holder}

Scoring_Aid.detail:
Victory Points
The player placing cubes receives VP for the following:

• 1 VP for each city a cube is placed in while expanding (2 VP for Atlanta).
• 1 VP for each new cube color that the expanding railroad has not been connected to previously.
• 1 VP if the railroad involved in the expansion has just been chartered.
• 1 VP if the newly chartered company is the VC, the M&C, or the GM&O (denoted on the Charter    share with a + near the railroad name).

gameplay_Aid.detail:
Actions
The current player must choose one of the following:

Charter a Railroad
• Auction an available Charter share (gold ones from the bottom rows).
• Winner uses most plentiful color. If tied, then choose a color from those tied.
• Put cube on Charter share.
• Build track and score VP (see Railroad Expansion and Scoring).
• If two cities can't be connected, action is treated as pass. No track is built and money is   returned.
• If all colors are on map, game is over. Player with most VP wins.

Auction Common Share
• Auction an available Common share (visible silver ones from the bottom rows).
• Build track and score VP (see Railroad Expansion and Scoring).
• The corresponding Charter share will be diluted.
• Common Share Marker will advance, pay dividends if applicable (every second auctioned   share).

Presidential Expansion
• Can only be done by the player who holds the Charter share of that company.
• Build track and score VP (see Railroad Expansion and Scoring).

Pass
• Take no action(s). If all players pass consecutively, game is over. Player with most VP wins.

auction_charter_Aid.detail:
Building Track
Place cubes on the map for $3 each, which is paid to the bank. The money comes from the winning bid except in the "Presidential Expansion", where you use your personal money.

• A railroad can't start in a city that has no other cubes, except for the 8 railroads that start the   game as available. Those companies are denoted by gold letters in the map.
• Only one cube can be in each rural intersection.
• Cubes can't be placed in a city or rural intersection occupied by or adjacent to a cube of the   same color belonging to another railroad.
• All cubes (except the one in their home city) must be adjacent to a cube of that railroad already   in the board.
• Cubes of the color matching the one on the Charter share must be used.
• Cubes are in a limited quantity.
• If a cube is placed in one of the starting cities of a railroad not yet available, put its shares in   the bottom rows.

info.name: Antebellum Rails

info.description: Build the South’s first rail networks, compete for cities, and shape the region’s industrial future in the years before the Civil War.
info.ruleText:
Starting Money

  • 3 Players: $40 

  • 4 Players: $30

  • 5 Players: $24


Minimum Bid: $0

First Action: Charter SC


Actions

  • Charter a Railroad

    • Auction

    • Majority color

    • Cube on charter

    • Connect 2+ cities

    • Build track and score VP

    • Illegal build: (no 2 cities, color already in home city) == Pass

  • Auction a Common Share

    • Auction

    • Dilute Charter Share (to $3)

    • No requirements

    • Build track and score VP

    • Move Common Stock Sale Marker

    • If in 2: Pay Dividends and reset

  • Presidential Expansion

    • Only Charter holder using personal money

    • Build track and score VP

  • Pass


Railroad Expansion

  • $3 each

  • Need a cube already there (except initial companies)

  • Only 1 cube in Rural

  • Same color never adjacent or together

  • One continuous line (branching is allowed)

  • Common Share / Presidential Expansion with same color

  • Cubes are limited


Scoring

  • 1 VP for city (2 for Atlanta)

  • 1 VP for new color

  • 1 VP for chartering

  • 1 VP extra for VC, M&C or GM&O

  • Make new railroads available


Ending / Winning the Game

  • Purple cube on the map or all pass consecutively

  • Most VP win


For an exhaustive explanation, click the "rules overview link"
info.helpText:
• Take your seats
  • Click Setup to choose between "base game" and "expansion 1"
  • First turn is always the auction of SC Charter share (if none buys it, it counts as a Pass and next player can freely choose their action
  • You can click on a share to see its home cities highlighted in the map
  • To start an Auction, drag the desired Share into the "Current Auction box"
  • Select your bid with "+", "-"  or clicking the amount and "bid" or "pass"
  • Build track dragging cubes from the supply to the map
  • Drag new available shares to the bottom row using the green dot
  • Every second auctioned Common share, a Pay Dividends button will appear
  • You can always check the log on the upper right, or even the full history
  • There are 3 player aids you can consult at any time, right under the log

phase1.detail:
: If this icon is showing in the top right, that means at least one other player is modifying a value (not necessarily the same as you) at the same time. This does not affect your value changes, but a notice won't be added to the history until all players have closed the modify value window. Click on the icon to forcibly close the modify value window for all players except for yourself.

Update button: Closes this window and updates your values according to the input change. This change will not show up in the history box until all players have closed the modify value window.

Change by: You may type a positive or negative value here, and this difference will automatically apply to the 'Set to' value next to it.

Set Total to: You may type any value here to indicate the total value, and this will automatically calculate the difference and show this as the 'Change by' value next to it.

+/- buttons: Click these buttons to add or subtract 1/5 value from the 'Set Total to' & 'Change by' values.

thenRoutine.note: This stops an update print when resetting a SET counter

Check this box and click the green button to reset the game.
